Good grief!

title: If you need to speak to a human, call 1800walmart, survive the AI rep, then you will get transferred
text: I ordered curtain rods. They were not delivered.  The online site indicated delivery had been attempted . No further explanation.   Three days later they still were not here so I called the local store.    The local store could not access my online order.   They had no knowledge of an attempted delivery .  They could not even tell me if the items were in stock at the store.      they did give me the telephone number to call walmart.     The AI rep is frustrating,   She asks the same questions over and over and did not want to transfer me to a person but eventually did so.   The nice gentle man in india or pakistan was able to tell me my best option was to cancel the order and he gave me a discount code to apply to the order.   Interestingly, the AI rep told me my order was not eligible for cancellation.     It was nice to reach a human who was able to fix the problem but it took a lot of my time; searching the online site, calling the local store and working through 1-800 walmart’s AI to reach a human does not encourage me to order from walmart in the future.

title: Inconsistent Inconveniences, daily.
text: I cannot wrap my head around how the biggest retailer on planet Earth somehow cannot make an app that behaves like it was designed by people who have used a phone before.  The Walmart app is the software equivalent of taking one step forward, two steps backward, then breaking into some little line dance nobody asked for.  iOS has supported clear and tinted widgets for over a year, and Walmart is still standing in the corner eating drywall, either refusing to play along or completely unable to figure out how.  And don’t even get me started on the actual app UI. One day the widget works like a normal civilized piece of technology, and the next day I’m standing at a gas pump, slowly losing my will to get gas, digging through the app for five full minutes trying to find wherever they buried the fuel discount code because the widget decided to stop doing its job.  This is the only app on my phone that I use daily and somehow also resent daily. It is annoying, frustrating, inconsistent, and somehow feels like it is holding my phone hostage.  Walmart clearly has the money, the resources, the engineers, the data, the everything. Yet the app still feels like it was assembled during a tornado by raccoons with Jira tickets.  At this point, it feels less like Walmart forgot about good UI and more like Walmart saw modern iOS support, looked directly at it, and said, “No.”

I asked to speak with a manager and was told I was at the highest point. I then asked for the corporate number and was told they don't give the corporate number out. Scamming people all day. When you call to inquire about the refund they will tell you that they have no record of it and then tell you it will be up to 10 business days for a refund again. They definitely take your money immediately but want you to jump through hoops to get a refund. In order for me to get a refund sooner I definitely have to contact my financial institution because Walmart is definitely scamming.

text: I gave a five-star review at self-checkout because the worker was kind and helpful. After paying, I selected “no receipt” on the machine, assuming that would be fine.  Unfortunately, as I was leaving with only three items, I was stopped and asked to show a receipt. This caused unnecessary anxiety and embarrassment. Luckily, I had the receipt on my phone, but the experience left me wondering why customers are offered a “no receipt” option if they may still be stopped and asked to produce one at the exit.  If receipts are required to leave the store, the machine should make that clear before giving customers the option to decline one.
